review_title: Quizlet is slowly taking over my entire study plan, I am obsessing over it!
description: So far it makes my study for Amazon Web Services way better since I can repurpose my inputs as standard flash cards, match each term and even game-ify each question.
author: Armando R.
pros: I've been extracting from article writing related topics in Amazon Web Services to gain my certification and to study them. This allows me to push further my content creation when it comes to flash cards and also with my youtube channel to document how I use Quizlet. Text generation is straightforward since I can take extracts from the modules and use them as flashcards. Voice and text from what Ive seen in paid tier makes it more immerssive to further advance the way I study to sharpen my knowledge pe module. Image generation is still a work in progress because I haven't tried it yet.
cons: My only concern is that perhaps you can't see if you can switch from Spaced Repetition to standard study approach.
author_duration: Used the software for: I used a free trial

description: My main use of this software is using the flashcards and matching feature. We done use the rest.
author: Oneida O.
pros: I really like that students can practice using the flashcards and listening to it being read.
cons: I am not impressed with the spelling feature or the gravity one. My student love the matching and enjoy beating their scores.

description: Really good program in terms of ease of access, but the worst for not sharing information freely
author: Margaret W.
pros: It is cheaper than paper and can have pictures integrated in flashcard
cons: They put a paywall on everything useful and it is pretty frustrating
author_duration: Used the software for: I used a free trial

description: My overall experience with Quizlet has been great. Quizlet helped me a lot throughout the years and has been a great use of my money.
author: Cylei H.
pros: What I like most about Quizlet is its content creation. Quizlet is very good for studying and a great value for money.
cons: What I like least about Quizlet is how sometimes there can be a loading problem with the software itself.

review_title: Not so good for the free version
description: I start using it to learn french and in the begining it help me a lot because i was only studying vacubalary. But in the with the passing of time when i wanted to do more complex card i can't so i have to use other software for that poruse
author: Daniel R.
pros: All the features are easy to use. I love that they include the funtion to search other users study sets so you can use it too. Also all the study modes that it have really help you changing your study type.
cons: A lot of the most interesting features that it have are restricted to the premium version so for simple study cards its okay but if you want something more elaborated ones you will be very limited.

review_title: One of the better flashcard systems out there.
author: Kimberly L.
pros: The flashcard system is a great memorization tool. I love that it provides a variety of ways to ensure you remember something.
cons: Only words well for DOK1 and DOK2 questions. Once you need to see more critical thinking or ensure that someone can solve a process, it breaks down.

description: Great! Its easy to maneuver, and makes studying easy and fun. I used it to memorize the menu at my waitressing job!
author: Kaitlyn H.
pros: I can scan documents to make a personalized study set! It also makes studying fun! I enjoy it a lot.
cons: There's not much to not like! But i wish i could use the learn section more without needing premium.

description: I love Quizlet and recommend it to everyone. I've used it for about a decade now and it was as great when I first downloaded it as it is now.
author: Brianna U.
pros: User interface is very intuitive, I appreciate the different options to display the quizzes, so much better than flipping over a paper flash card.
cons: I haven't found anything to not work well for me, but I also don't really use all the features of Quizlet, so not sure how the more advanced/in-depth things work.

description: I have had a super good experience with quizlet. It is so helpful and a very good study tool.
author: Rachael N.
pros: I absolutely love quizlet. I especially love their new feature that has a check or an x and you swipe one way to say if you know it or not. That has been very helpful for my studying.
cons: It can be hard to find other people's quizlets. It may be helpful if you could 'follow' others so you could easily navigate to their quizlets.

description: My overall experience is 10/10. I highly recommend using this platform to study.
author: Jamilia J.
pros: I use it to study for my Music Therapy Exam. It has several testing options that people upload.
cons: There are no cons. When you have the yearly Subscription; you have access to everything.

review_title: Quizlet is a must-have for teachers!
description: I like creating entertaining, engaging, and interactive activities that are easily accessible for my students.
author: Melissa M.
pros: I like how easy it is to add a class, create study guides, and make fun assessments.
cons: I don't really have anything that I don't like...and I have the free version.

description: Quizlet has been a game changer in my classroom. I love the excitement it brings into our classroom, along with the great practice.
author: Michelle Z.
pros: Quizlet is easy and fast to use, and the students love using it. The best part is it is a great tool to help students review vocabulary! As a teacher, I love the Quizlet Live part of the tool. The excitement it brings is amazing!
cons: I don't have anything negative to say about Quizlet.

description: Quizlet has so many benefits and I believe all language courses should incorporate it somehow. it is innovative and interactive and keeps students motivated.
author: Katherine Marie H.
pros: The most impactful feature is how the flashcards can be learned not just as a flashcard but also as other games and formats and it helps to keep the words in a random order making sure all the words are studied properly. I also like how fun it is and how it keeps track of the progress to give a good idea of how the students are doing with the new vocabulary.
cons: The "live mode" requires at least 2 plus players but sometimes you are just tutoring one student and so it would be fun to also use the feature with just one player to give another alternative way of testing the student's progress.

review_title: A masterpiece of e-learning for me
description: I use it daily and I have had over 2000 sets. I am enjoying it a lot as a teacher and we are using in our online school now. All students love it. A few might not learn something effectively based on our empirical research findings but this is around %5. %95 learn, have fun, encouraged and inspired by Quizlet
author: Dogus A.
pros: Its gamification features A customized learning path and user-friendliness Being able to follow all the progress as a teacher Their customer service is perfect to respond to all your needs
cons: Social learning can be improved since Quizlet live is not that good enough so classrooms cannot make use of it as much as Kahoot and Quizizz.

review_title: Fast and easy way to use Flashcard quizzes and to study!
description: Great! Not much to critique it on. I have been using it since I was a child and now that I'm teaching other kids/students, I have passed it on to them!
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: It is very easy for both teachers and students to use. My students use it for vocabulary practicing on a weekly basis (two times a week at least) and their improvement is usually pretty clear. There are different ways and games that help students learn and the system remembers which terms they need more help on (starred terms) and will repeat those more often. It doesn't take me too long to make a Quizlet which I find very convenient and time saving! My students tell me they make quizzes for themselves and find the process of manually creating the quizzes helpful and easy because it helps them learn the content. The flashcard feature was great because when you tapped the flashcard, the definition or the explanation would show. It helps a lot with studying.
cons: Quizlet used to be a completely free app, now it is requiring users pay for features that used to be free. While the membership fee isn't a lot compared to other fees, it is still a fee and some students prefer not to use Quizlet to its fullest extent because of it. Nothing else I would criticize Quizlet for!

description: Overall this has been a fantastic tool for my husband in grad school and my kindergartener and second grader learning sight works for multiple languages. Highly reccomend!
author: Susan U.
pros: My husband used Quizlet during his stint with higher edu action, and we’ve carried it over into helping our kids learn. I love how easy it is to fire off a set of flash cards for anything! Quick and free, saving having to buy and fill out a bunch of index cards.
cons: Sometimes it’s a little glitchy, but overall we have only had great experiences with Quizlet.

description: My students really improved with the practice on Quizlet. I could see the progress with test scores and with everyday vocabulary usage.
author: Carissa S.
pros: This was a great tool for learning and practicing vocabulary in my class. Students liked this program to study at home.
cons: This is great for practice, but could use features that help individualize for each student.

description: Quizlet is a great tool that allows me the learner to learn and retain new knowledge for a reasonably low price.
author: Teddy D.
pros: In Quizlet, there are many modes in which the learner can study a topic in various methods of learners
cons: Anyone is allowed to create a study set and there is no bot that reviews and corrects the information given. This can create misinformation for the learner when utilizing the study set on Quizlet.

review_title: A classic learning software for a reason!
description: Technical writing involves very specific terms, so I can easily memorize them with Quizlet. I also use Quizlet to share my flash cards easily with co-workers.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Quizlet makes it possible to carry flash cards anywhere, and to review on any device. The interface is great and intuitive. In my opinion, this is an essential software for teachers, or anyone wishing to memorize terms. It's highly useful for learning languages and technical terms.
cons: The learn features can be lacking if you don't pay for a subscription. The live feature also requires purchase.

description: My overall experience with Quizlet has been great. I've been studying and learning with Quizlet for years and I can say in my eyes this is the BEST learning tool to give your kids or yourself. It's very easy to set up. What makes it even easier and more fun is that it's set up like a game. It's got music in the back and you can choose your time that you get on each question. It's amazing.
author: alexis R.
pros: What I like most about Quizlet is that it takes something so simple as a big white piece of paper, makes it digital, writes the questions and answers, and helps us stick them in our brains for long-term use. I've used it for about 2+ years for academics and music. I can say there are not many test that I have taken and didn't pass using Quizlet. Strongly recommended.
cons: What I least like about Quizlet is that sometimes on the mobile version it glitches and gets stuck. So because of that I have got some answers wrong or have seen some answers to early and that kinda spoiled the learning experience for me. I would sometimes have to reload the page for it to act right.

description: As a student, finding a learning style that suits you is a challenge. Quizlet has been a game changer and it has definitely helped me maintain a good standard of work.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Quizlet is easy to use with a very clean, user friendly interface . It enhances studying by enabling users to create their own flashcards and various quizzes. It also promotes asynchronous learning which is a huge bonus especially during this time of online classes.
cons: Advertisements could be distracting but all in all, a good app.

author: Robin E.
pros: In truth quizlet can be used for any subject, but as a language teacher I find it an excellent activity for my students. It allows them to review new vocabulary and practice it until mastery. There are independent activities and the fun quizlet live competitive option which can be played in teams or individually , especially useful when teaching remotely.
cons: I would like the ability to use it more easily with Hebrew and be able to put in the vowels to help worker readers

review_title: Quizlet is a multifunctional software, I love it for school purposes
description: Overall quizlet is a great study tool. I’ve used it for years now — through high school and college — to make my own study sets or utilize sets made by other users. It is easy to navigate / use, and the free account has a ton of different uses — I never felt the need to pay for a premium account!
author: Rachel S.
pros: Very user friendly. I like the ability to create folders and sets of flash cards within those folders. I love the multiple options there are to learn study sets, from practice quizzes to matching etc. I also love the ability to access other users’ study sets if I don’t feel like making my own. Finally I love the ability to add pictures to cards!
cons: It is best for straightforward vocab, terms and study concepts rather than bigger more complex concepts. So to make flash cards for bigger paragraphs and ideas is challenging and not as helpful just because the setup of quizlet doesn’t allow as much room with that kind of thing (I.e. limited organizational means such as adding bullet points - major drawback!).

description: The heavyweight of online learning
author: Craig B.
pros: Accelerates learning by adding in the competition factor, individualized and team leaning.
cons: They took away gravity. Students liked it.

review_title: Quizlet makes studying more enjoyable and organized
author: Carsyn D.
pros: I liked that quizlet keeps everything in one place. Rather than having flash cards to look through it’s much easier to look at them online and not have to keep up with a physical copy.
cons: I don’t really have a complaint for quizlet. I use quizlet plus and feel the price is fair.
